message:
feat(simd_scan): wire ansi_state_step into all scan variants + 8 regression tests (ft-nk6u9)

Follow-on to 99703277 which added the StringPhase state machine and
the ansi_state_step helper. This commit migrates the four scan
variants to actually call the unified helper and pins the corrected
behaviour with 8 regression tests exercising the previously-broken
byte-counting paths.

## Variant migration (scan_newlines_and_ansi_scalar +
## scan_newlines_and_ansi_scalar_with_state)

Both scalar paths replaced a hand-rolled CSI-only state machine with
a call into ansi_state_step. Before:

    if b == 0x1b {
        in_escape = true;
        ansi_byte_count += 1;
    } else if in_escape {
        ansi_byte_count += 1;
        if (0x40..=0x7E).contains(&b) && b != b'[' {
            in_escape = false;
        }
    }

The `(0x40..=0x7E)` terminator range incorrectly matched the string
introducer bytes ] P X ^ _, so the scanner would clear in_escape
immediately after seeing any of them and count the entire string body
as plain text. After:

    if ansi_state_step(b, &mut in_escape, &mut string_phase) {
        ansi_byte_count += 1;
    }

The helper correctly routes string introducers into StringPhase::Body
and keeps counting until BEL or ESC\.

scan_newlines_and_ansi_scalar_with_state now carries string_phase
through state so cross-chunk callers (the streaming path used by the
ingest pipeline) resume an in-flight OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC body correctly
across chunk boundaries. Commit stores string_phase back into state
alongside in_escape and pending_utf8_continuations.

## 8 regression tests (all tagged ft_nk6u9_)

  1. osc_bel_terminator_counts_full_sequence
     Fixture: ESC ] 0 ; window title BEL after
     Pre-fix byte count: 2. Post-fix: 17. Asserts density > 0.7 to
     pin the downstream density heuristic too.

  2. osc_st_terminator_counts_full_sequence
     Fixture: ESC ] 52 ; c ; base64payload ESC \
     Pin the OSC-with-ST terminator path (same sequence used by
     OSC 52 clipboard writes).

  3. dcs_sequence_counts_full_body
     Fixture: a real Sixel-ish DCS header + body + ST
     Used by Sixel / ReGIS / DECRQSS consumers; rare but well-defined.

  4. pm_and_apc_sequences_count_full_body
     Fixtures: ESC ^ payload ESC \ and ESC _ Gf=32,payload ESC \
     Asserts ansi_byte_count == full input length (the whole sequence
     is ANSI; no trailing plain bytes).

  5. osc_body_preserves_newline_count
     Fixture: plain newline + OSC containing a newline + plain newline
     Asserts newline_count == 3 regardless of OSC state. The OSC path
     must not suppress newline counting — it's an orthogonal axis.

  6. osc_body_split_across_chunks
     Stateful scanner test: first chunk ends mid-OSC-body, second
     chunk contains the BEL. Asserts state.string_phase progresses
     from Body → None and that the BEL terminator is correctly
     counted in the second chunk.

  7. osc_st_split_between_esc_and_backslash
     Hard case: chunk boundary falls between the two bytes of the
     ESC \ String Terminator. First chunk ends with ESC, second
     starts with \. Pins StringPhase::SawEsc carry across chunks —
     without that carry, the second chunk would count the `\` as
     a plain byte.

  8. csi_sequence_still_terminates_on_final_byte +
     single_char_c1_still_terminates
     Negative controls: CSI (ESC [ 1 ; 31 m) and single-char C1
     (ESC M, ESC D) paths MUST NOT regress. The string-intro branch
     is a new arm on the state machine, not a replacement for the
     existing CSI path.

## retry.rs test: telemetry() now returns a struct requiring .snapshot()

The ft-gc4hz cancellation regression test in the prior commit accessed
`circuit.telemetry().failures_recorded` directly. Another agent's
concurrent work changed `telemetry()` to return a handle that needs
`.snapshot()` called on it for the actual TelemetrySnapshot. Update
the test to call `.snapshot()` once and read all three fields from
the bound tel variable (also adds an assertion on trips_total == 0
for belt-and-braces — a cancelled op must neither open the circuit
nor count as a trip).

## Beads

Minor metadata updates — no net status changes beyond the closures
already landed in this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
